extraction.one_line_summary: CONTADOR Alberto attacked solo on the Alto de l'Angliru final climb of his career, caught breakaway and won while FROOME Chris controlled GC behind.
tactical_patterns: CONTADOR Alberto's symbolic attack on career-ending mountain stage succeeded because Team Sky prioritized GC defense over stage hunting.
NIBALI Vincenzo cracked 2km from Angliru summit despite second place GC, revealing altitude/gradient threshold weakness late in Grand Tours.
ZAKARIN Ilnur maintained third overall without attacking FROOME, suggesting teams concede Sky control when leader has sufficient margin.
Wet conditions caused crashes to DE LA CRUZ David and others in valley before climb, making positioning critical before final ascent.

rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: positive: Attacked and won Angliru on final career mountain stage, showing sustained third-week aggression despite not contending GC podium
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Team Sky controlled race pace with POELS Wout support, maintaining GC lead without responding to symbolic CONTADOR attack
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: Lost second overall by cracking 2km from Angliru summit, exposing vulnerability on steepest gradients in third week
rider_signals.ZAKARIN Ilnur: neutral: Secured third overall without attacking, conservative tactics when trailing by manageable margin
rider_signals.DE LA CRUZ David: negative: Crashed on wet roads before final climb while in second GC, poor bike handling in crucial positioning phase
rider_signals.POELS Wout: positive: Provided crucial Sky domestique support to FROOME on Angliru, demonstrating sustained climbing strength late in three-week race
